channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Seven dead, scores missing in Kyiv as Russia continues onslaught
item.description: Russia launches hundreds of drones at Ukraine in the third straight day of widespread attacks on targets across the country.

media:group.media:description: Residents of a Kyiv apartment building were seen in tears after Thursday's attack.

item.title: Latvian PM resigns in row over stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: Drones launched by Ukraine that were jammed by Russia either flew over or crashed in the Baltic country's territory, with local residents critical of the government's response.

media:group.media:description: Evika Silina ahs resigned as Latvia's prime minister after members of her coalition withdrew support over the sacking of her defence minister.

item.title: ICC fugitive flees Philippine Senate after shooting chaos
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity, had been holed up inside the Philippine Senate since Monday to avoid arrest. His whereabouts is now unknown.

media:group.media:description: Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa has fled the Senate, where he was hiding out from arrest.

item.title: Leadership contenders edge towards challenging UK PM Starmer
item.description: The UK's health secretary has resigned from cabinet and another Labour MP has quit parliament paving the way for a potential leadership challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

media:group.media:description: Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham are tipped to challenge UK prime minister Sir Keir Starmer.

item.title: 'Make a choice': Taylor defends comments aimed at non-citizens
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has defended his comments aimed at non-citizens as necessary and practical during a heated interview on 7.30.
item.dc:creator: Paul Johnson

media:group.media:description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor defended comments he made in his budget in reply speech on 7.30.

item.title: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
item.description: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: Shaun Mannagh kicked three goals in five minutes in the third quarter.

item.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win
item.description: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Queensland was awarded the game-tying try in the last minute before the bunker intervened.

item.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of 'Sammy' the seal
item.description: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.
item.dc:creator: Ethan White

media:group.media:description: Sammy the seal was often spotted by locals sleeping in several locations around Victoria's Mornington Peninsula.

item.title: Green light for Andrew Forrest's $70m Ningaloo Reef resort
item.description: After almost a decade of planning, mining billionaire Andrew Forrest's plan to build an eco-resort on the shores of the Ningaloo Reef has received the go-ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alistair Bates

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the Exmouth resort's hillside villas.

item.title: Labor's 'jaw dropping' fracking decision wedges One Nation at first opportunity
item.description: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: Premier Peter Malinauskas is pushing to end the moratorium on fracking in One Nation MP Jason Virgo's electorate of MacKillop.

item.title: Business groups lament migration plan as Taylor speaks
item.description: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but businesses say the economy needs a "sustainable" migration program.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: The Coalition wants to tie migration to housing supply.

item.title: Bosisto's to triple eucalyptus oil production with new distillery
item.description: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.
item.dc:creator: Justine Longmore and Warwick Long

media:group.media:description: Tegan Abbott says the new distillery will help Bosisto's triple its eucalyptus oil production.

item.title: Court hears of horror stabbing that offender blames on Ozempic
item.description: An Illawarra woman stabbed multiple times by her mother-in-law has told a court she continues to be haunted by images of the roadside attack.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: The offender, who cannot be named for legal reasons, told the court she does not remember stabbing her daughter-in-law and grandson.

item.title: Bus rollover on Queensland's Bruce Highway leaves one dead and many injured
item.description: Two other passengers suffered life-threatening injuries after the large coach overturned between Townsville and Bowen.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: The large coach crashed at Gumlu, between Townsville and Bowen.

item.title: Who is in Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing.

item.title: The key takeaways from Trump and Xi's meeting in China
item.description: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.
item.dc:creator: Allyson Horn, Lucia Stein, and Emily Clark

media:group.media:description: Mr Trump and Mr Xi's summit is focused on trade, regional security, and strengthening bilateral ties between the world's two largest economies.

item.title: Albanese taunts Taylor as another defector joins Labor ranks
item.description: After failing to catch anyone by surprise with the budget, Anthony Albanese had another trick up his sleeve.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Tammy Tyrrell made a surprise move to defect from the crossbench to Labor.

item.title: 'A good fit': How ex-Lambie senator found a home with Labor
item.description: She won office running under Jacqui Lambie's banner, but brand-new Labor recruit Tasmanian senator Tammy Tyrrell says she wanted a "seat at the powerful table of the government".
item.dc:creator: Adam Langenberg

item.title: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to co-headline World Cup final half-time show
item.description: The first-ever FIFA World Cup final half-time show has been announced.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jewell

media:group.media:description: Shakira will co-headline alongside Madonna and BTS.

item.title: Police search abandoned mine shafts for missing woman Trisha Graf
item.description: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.
item.dc:creator: Declan Durrant and Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: Major Crime investigators were on scene in Andamooka today and yesterday.

item.title: Ronda Rousey back for redemption after being burnt out
item.description: UFC Hall of Famer Ronda Rousey is returning to the ring after almost a decade away, but this time she will be fighting on her own terms.

media:group.media:description: Ronda Rousey is returning to the ring in Los Angeles this weekend.

item.title: 'Foldable' pistols, 3D-printed guns seized in raid 450m from police station
item.description: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.
item.dc:creator: James Vyver

media:group.media:description: A 39‑year‑old Belconnen man faced court this morning.

item.title: 'Like driving on bubble wrap': Residents describe mouse plague horror
item.description: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.
item.dc:creator: Kenith Png, Joanna Prendergast, and Nadia Mitsopoulos

media:group.media:description: Farmer Robert Mitchell scoops up dead mice from his shed.

item.title: Antarctic expeditioner removed after weapon incident
item.description: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: A member of a Korean Antarctic research team is accused of threatening fellow crew members with a knife at Jang Bogo Station.

item.title: MPs join calls for mayor in Melbourne to resign over character reference saga
item.description: The mayor of Wyndham City Council, Preet Singh, has faced protests and calls to quit after he wrote a character reference for a man convicted of child sex offences.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Paul

media:group.media:description: Federal MP Joanne Ryan and state MP John Lister have both spoken in parliament about Preet Singh's refusal to resign as mayor of Wyndham.
